generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary actuator for motion control in the MABI MAX 100 serial robot, operating within defined maximum working range and achieving controlled rotational speeds via closed-loop feedback."
]

instance GearTransmissionSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical component providing torque amplification and speed reduction for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot's motion control system."
]

instance SiemensSinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial controller responsible for motion planning and real-time command execution in the MABI MAX 100 serial robot system."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance JointPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor capturing position data for all six axes of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointPositionData
]

instance JointVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor capturing velocity data for all six axes of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointVelocityData
]

instance JointTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor capturing torque data for all six axes of the MABI MAX 100 serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointTorqueData
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing environmental temperature data for the manufacturing automation domain."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureData
]

instance OperationalLoadSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing operational load metrics for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLoadData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Data Transmission
instance JointPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointPositionSensor
]

instance JointVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointVelocitySensor
]

instance JointTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointTorqueSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance OperationalLoadData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLoadSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model describing the MABI MAX 100 robot's functional decomposition across requirements, functional, and logical layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SimulinkMotionControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model representing the closed-loop motion control system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL-based component model describing the MABI MAX 100 robot's physical components, interfaces, and connectors."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLToolchain
]

instance JointPositionDataAggregation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of joint position measurements from the MABI MAX 100 serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingPipeline
]

instance JointVelocityDataAggregation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of joint velocity measurements from the MABI MAX 100 serial robot."
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingPipeline
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of environmental parameters (temperature, humidity) for the manufacturing domain."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingPipeline
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message Queuing Telemetry Transport protocol enabling real-time or near-real-time data acquisition from physical sensors to digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataIngestionService, DataVisualizationService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling tool supporting architecture modeling across requirements, functional, logical, and product layers."
    DTDFVocab:enables ArchitectureModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for behavioral modeling and closed-loop control system simulation."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionControlSimulationService
]

instance AADLToolchain : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Toolchain supporting AADL-based component modeling for physical components, interfaces, and connectors."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentModelingService
]

instance DataPreprocessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software pipeline performing validation, filtering, and preprocessing of sensor data (noise reduction, unit normalization)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataQualityAssuranceService
]

// C6: Services
instance SensorDataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and abstracting contextual data traces from physical sensors via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides SensorDataAggregationInsight
]

instance ArchitectureModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance MotionControlSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance ComponentModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance DataQualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

]

]

instance ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SiemensSinumerik840DslController,
                 PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor,
                 GearTransmissionSystem,
                 JointPositionSensor,
                 JointVelocitySensor,
                 JointTorqueSensor,
                 EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor,
                 OperationalLoadSensor

instance DigitalShadowEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel,
                 SimulinkMotionControlModel,
                 AADLComponentModel,
                 JointPositionDataAggregation,
                 JointVelocityDataAggregation,
                 EnvironmentalConditionData,

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** between physical and digital components through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which often involve **multi-level abstraction** (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers). While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed, the integration of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML (including Internal Block Diagrams, IBDs) and **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods [29, 30]) implies real-time or near-real-time data flows for behavioral modeling. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) suggests a **one-way data flow** from physical to digital, but a Digital Twin (DT) can include **active components** to process and push data back, necessitating **temporal granularity** aligned with system operational needs (e.g., manufacturing processes). No specific time scales (e.g., Hz, ms) or service-level temporal constraints (e.g., 99.9% uptime) are provided, but the use of **state machines** (SysML v1.7) and **automata** (MontiArc) indicates discrete-event or event-driven temporal modeling."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration is framed within **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks, leveraging **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML (IBDs), AADL, MontiArc, or ACME** to model **component-connector systems** with explicit interfaces and interconnections. The **RFLP methodology** (Requirements-Functions-Logics-Physics) [18, 19] provides a structured **orchestration pattern** for hierarchical system decomposition. **Component integration** is achieved via **heterogeneous toolchains** (e.g., SysML + Simulink + C++/Java), while **resource management** is implied through **multi-level abstraction** to handle complexity. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Fig. 1) introduces a **UML class diagram** for digital shadows, but no explicit **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., event buses, API gateways) are detailed. The paper suggests **active DT components** (e.g., simulation engines) can enable bidirectional data flow, but no architecture patterns (e.g., service-oriented, peer-to-peer) are specified."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. The lifecycle governance integrates heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives and involves iterative refinement across tools like **SysML, MathWorks Simulink, or general-purpose languages (C++, Java)**. The **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** methodology explicitly structures lifecycle phases by aligning physical system behaviors with digital representations, ensuring traceability from abstract requirements to executable models. State machines in **SysML v1.7** or **MontiArc Automaton** formalize behavioral transitions, while simulation tools (e.g., **Simulink**) validate dynamic system behaviors at later stages. Lifecycle governance is implicitly supported by **version control** (e.g., Git) and **quality assurance practices** tied to ADL compliance, though explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010) are not detailed in the document.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
Model fidelity is ensured through **multi-abstraction layer modeling** (requirements → functional → logical → product/technical layers) using **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES). **Validation** is achieved via **executable simulation models** (e.g., Simulink) that replicate expected physical behavior, enabling **behavioral verification** against design specifications. **Uncertainty quantification** is implied through the use of **active software components** to process sensor data and correct discrepancies, though explicit **error detection/correction mechanisms** (e.g., Kalman filters, anomaly detection algorithms) are not described. **Quality assurance processes** rely on **model-to-simulation traceability** and **interoperability validation** across heterogeneous tools (SysML, Simulink, C++/Java). However, **formal uncertainty sources** (e.g., sensor noise, model parameter drift) and **quantitative error metrics** are not explicitly detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not specify technical details regarding network infrastructure, protocols beyond MQTT, or latency/bandwidth requirements. The system is described as a **digital shadow**, which is a **passive entity** with a **unidirectional data flow** from physical to digital, lacking any mention of control flows, command execution, or bidirectional communication. Thus, no technical connection specifications (e.g., protocols, topology, security) are available."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems primarily through streamed data protocols (e.g., MQTT for real-time data flows) and general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java). While the document does not explicitly mention Asset Administration Shells (AAS) or legacy system integration, it highlights the use of SysML, Simulink, and other simulation tools as interoperability bridges. The one-way data flow characteristic of Digital Shadows (as opposed to active Digital Twins) implies dependencies on external systems for data aggregation and abstraction, but no explicit interoperability standards or integration patterns (e.g., OPC UA, IIoT frameworks) are detailed. Horizontal integration is implicitly supported by the modularity of ADLs and MBSE methodologies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The system adheres to **SysML** for systems modeling and **AADL** for architecture description, both standardized **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. **MQTT** is referenced as a protocol for data exchange (implied via broader IoT/DT contexts, though not explicitly named in the provided text). **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** are explicitly mentioned in the **Abstract and Index Terms** as a key standard/specification implemented in the system, though not detailed in the body text. No other **industry standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 23247, IEC 62424) or **interoperability frameworks** (e.g., OPC UA) are mentioned in the documents.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **MQTT-based data streaming** (as referenced in [52]) for real-time communication between physical assets and digital representations, ensuring low-latency data exchange critical for safety-critical operations. In the context of the **MABI robotic arm (Document [44])**, physical safety is addressed through a **safety cell**—a designated workspace equipped with light curtains and emergency stop mechanisms to prevent unauthorized access or collisions. While no explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) are detailed in the documents, the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS) framework (Documents [46], [47], [50])** emphasizes **structured data modeling** for traceability and accountability, which indirectly supports risk mitigation by enabling auditable data lineage. However, no formal **access control mechanisms**, **fail-safe protocols**, or **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., intrusion detection, zero-trust architectures) are specified in the provided sources."
]
